content.title: Architecture Overview
content.description:
Hobson uses a bootstrap architecture where a minimal HTML file loads a modular kernel from IndexedDB. This page aggregates the detailed documentation for each architectural component.

---

## The Kernel

![](item://c0c0c0c0-0001-0000-0000-000000000000#description)

---

## Bootstrap Process

![](item://c0c0c0c0-0002-0000-0000-000000000000#description)

---

## Kernel Modules

The kernel is decomposed into specialized subsystems:

```query
const modules = await api.query({ type: api.IDS.KERNEL_MODULE });
modules.sort((a, b) => (a.name || '').localeCompare(b.name || ''));
return transcludeEach(modules, m =>
  `### [${m.name}](item://${m.id})\n${m.content?.description?.split('\n')[0] || 'No description'}`,
  { chrome: 'none' }
);
```

## Why This Architecture?

| Problem | Solution |
|---------|----------|
| Monolithic kernel can't be linked to | Decompose into items |
| Kernel can't modify itself | Load from storage, not source file |
| Broken code crashes everything | Safe mode bypasses user code |
| Can't nest Hobson instances | ID prefixing isolates storage |

**Result**: The system increasingly defines itself in terms of itself.
